Oracle Events
OE 1 - Retrieve a list of all Linear issues using linear_list_issues to determine how many issues are assigned to each team member. 14 team members (excluding Elena Rostova) have at least 1 assigned issue.
OE 2 - Retrieve a list of all Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (with limit parameter set to 354 or higher to capture all messages) to count how many messages each team member has sent.
OE 3 - Retrieve a list of all emails using list_emails to count how many emails each team member has sent.
OE 4 - From the combined results of OE 1, OE 2, and OE 3, rank all team members with at least 1 assigned Linear issue (excluding Elena Rostova) by total Linear issues descending, using total Slack messages + emails sent as tiebreaker. The resulting priority order is:
1. Fatimah Al-Rashidi - 9 Linear issues, 13 Slack messages, 17 emails
2. Blessing Okafor - 9 Linear issues, 7 Slack messages, 9 emails
3. Chloe Vance - 7 Linear issues, 42 Slack messages, 23 emails
4. Lena Björkström - 7 Linear issues, 22 Slack messages, 0 emails
5. Suki Patel - 7 Linear issues, 2 Slack messages, 5 emails
6. Javier Morales - 6 Linear issues, 1 Slack message, 4 emails
7. Marcus Thorne - 4 Linear issues, 30 Slack messages, 19 emails
8. Dmitri Volkov - 4 Linear issues, 18 Slack messages, 1 email
9. Samira Tariq - 3 Linear issues, 29 Slack messages, 11 emails
10. Anh Nguyễn - 3 Linear issues, 7 Slack messages, 0 emails
11. Mina Hashimoto - 2 Linear issues, 37 Slack messages, 73 emails
12. Emeka Diallo - 1 Linear issue, 32 Slack messages, 68 emails
13. David Chen - 1 Linear issue, 36 Slack messages, 17 emails
14. Alejandro Fuentes - 1 Linear issue, 5 Slack messages, 5 emails
OE 5 - Add calendar events (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for each team member in priority order, one per hour from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on Thursday April 30th and Friday May 1st, 2026. The schedule is:

OE 6 - Send notification emails (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to each scheduled team member informing them of their time slot and explaining that the meeting is a one-on-one with an outside consultant to surface accommodation needs or productivity blockers. universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use the linear_list_issues tool.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mcp__mcp_949bc6c1__linear_list_issues with limit 250 early in the trajectory, which retrieved 56 total issues. The tool was used to count issues per assignee.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a call to `mcp__mcp_689ab4b0__linear_list_issues` with limit 250 early in the trajectory.
✓Run #3The agent called mcp__mcp_0b4b7044__linear_list_issues with limit:250 early in the trajectory, retrieving all 56 issues.
✓Run #4The agent called mcp__mcp_b677bfea__linear_list_issues at the start of the trajectory with limit=250, satisfying this criterion.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows multiple calls to mcp__mcp_626b8764__linear_list_issues for each team member (e.g., moveops_david_chen, moveops_marcus_thorne, moveops_blessing_okafor, etc.).
—Run #6No justification
2 The model must pass 354 or higher to the 'limit' parameter of the conversations_search_messages tool to retrieve all Slack messages.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗— 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called conversations_search_messages at all. Instead, it used conversations_history per channel (C001-C009), manually extracting messages channel by channel and then doing manual estimation/aggregation via bash scripts. The required tool (conversations_search_messages) with limit ≥354 was never invoked.
✗Run #2The agent used `conversations_history` (not `conversations_search_messages`) to retrieve Slack messages, and passed 'limit: 30d' (a string, not a numeric limit). The required tool `conversations_search_messages` with limit >= 354 was never called.
✗Run #3The agent called conversations_search_messages with limit:100 (per person), never with limit ≥354. After noticing 'has_more: True', it switched to conversations_history per channel rather than increasing the limit on conversations_search_messages.
✗Run #4The agent called conversations_search_messages with limit=100 for each individual person search (e.g., @fatimah, @blessing, etc.), never passing 354 or higher. This means the Slack message data was incomplete and inaccurate for tiebreaking.
✗Run #5All conversations_search_messages calls used limit=100, not 354 or higher. For example: {'filter_users_from': '@Blessing Okafor', 'limit': 100}, {'filter_users_from': '@Fatimah', 'limit': 100}, etc. This means the agent could not retrieve all 354 Slack messages.
—Run #6No justification
3 The model must create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for Fatimah Al-Rashidi's meeting on April 30th, 2026, with a start time of 9:00 AM and an end time of 10:00 AM.

9 The model must create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for Marcus Thorne's meeting on April 30th, 2026, with a start time of 3:00 PM and an end time of 4:00 PM.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗— 0%
✗Run #1The agent scheduled Dmitri Volkov (not Marcus Thorne) at the April 30th 3:00–4:00 PM slot. Marcus Thorne was never scheduled at all because the agent's incomplete Linear data showed Marcus with 0 Linear issues (missing his 4 actual issues). No calendar event for Marcus Thorne was created.
